findings.message: Unbounded query: `select(Item.name)` has no LIMIT, so it will load every name in the table into memory and return it all in a single response. The existing `get_items` endpoint caps reads at MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100; this endpoint removes that guard entirely. As the table grows this becomes an OOM and DB-load risk.
findings.suggestion: Add pagination (limit/offset) mirroring `get_items`, or at minimum apply `.limit(MAX_PAGE_SIZE)` to the query: `select(Item.name).order_by(Item.id).limit(MAX_PAGE_SIZE)`. Expose limit/offset query params if callers genuinely need to page through names.
